evals.prompt: Write a complete Python script that uses DALI dynamic mode to load and preprocess images for training an image classification model with PyTorch. The images are JPEGs on disk, and I need GPU-accelerated decode, resize to 224x224, and ImageNet normalization. The script should include the training loop.
evals.expected_output: Complete pipeline using ndd.readers.File, ndd.decoders.image(device='gpu'), ndd.resize, ndd.crop_mirror_normalize, .torch() handoff

assertions.name: correct-import
assertions.text: Uses import nvidia.dali.experimental.dynamic as ndd

assertions.name: reader-no-batchsize-in-constructor
assertions.text: batch_size is NOT passed to the ndd.readers.File() constructor (it belongs in next_epoch(), not the reader constructor)

assertions.name: reader-pascalcase
assertions.text: Reader is PascalCase: ndd.readers.File(...)

assertions.name: reader-stateful
assertions.text: Reader created once outside loop, reused across epochs

assertions.name: next-epoch-iteration
assertions.text: Uses reader.next_epoch(batch_size=N) for iteration

assertions.name: device-gpu-not-mixed
assertions.text: Uses device='gpu' for decoder, NOT device='mixed'

assertions.name: no-pipeline-mode
assertions.text: No pipeline-mode constructs (no @pipeline_def, pipe.build(), pipe.run()) and operators called directly on ndd (e.g. ndd.resize, not fn.resize or ndd.fn.resize)

assertions.name: torch-handoff
assertions.text: Uses .torch() for PyTorch conversion

assertions.name: no-unnecessary-evaluate
assertions.text: No unnecessary .evaluate() calls

evals.prompt: I have a Batch of 2D random values in DALI dynamic mode and need to extract the first column as crop_x and the second column as crop_y to pass to an operator. How do I do this? Show a working code example.
evals.expected_output: Uses batch.slice[0] and batch.slice[1] for samplewise slicing

assertions.name: correct-slice-usage
assertions.text: Uses batch.slice[0] and batch.slice[1] (not batch.select())

assertions.name: no-getitem
assertions.text: Does not use batch[0] or batch[:, 0] (Batch has no __getitem__)

assertions.name: correct-slice-semantics
assertions.text: Correctly explains that .slice indexes within each sample, not across samples

assertions.name: batch-size-to-random
assertions.text: Passes batch_size to ndd.random.uniform()

evals.prompt: Convert the following pipeline-mode DALI code to dynamic mode. Write the complete converted script.
evals.expected_output: Correct conversion with all pipeline-mode patterns replaced

assertions.name: device-gpu-not-mixed
assertions.text: device='mixed' converted to device='gpu'

assertions.name: reader-pascalcase
assertions.text: fn.readers.file converted to ndd.readers.File (PascalCase)

assertions.name: no-pipeline-mode
assertions.text: No pipeline-mode constructs (no @pipeline_def, pipe.build(), pipe.run()) and operators called directly on ndd (e.g. ndd.rotate, not fn.rotate or ndd.fn.rotate)

assertions.name: next-epoch-iteration
assertions.text: Uses reader.next_epoch(batch_size=N) for iteration (batch_size in next_epoch, not reader constructor)

assertions.name: seed-handling
assertions.text: Pipeline seed converted to ndd.random.set_seed() or RNG(seed=)

assertions.name: set-num-threads
assertions.text: Pipeline num_threads converted to ndd.set_num_threads()

assertions.name: batch-size-to-random
assertions.text: batch_size passed to random operators (uniform, coin_flip)

evals.prompt: My data loading code built with DALI's dynamic (imperative) API produces wrong results intermittently — images sometimes appear corrupted. The code decodes JPEG images on the GPU, resizes them, and normalizes them. How do I debug this? Write a debugging guide with code examples.
evals.expected_output: Recommends EvalMode.sync_full or sync_cpu for debugging, explains async execution model, code examples use correct dynamic mode patterns

assertions.name: recommends-sync-mode
assertions.text: Recommends EvalMode.sync_full or EvalMode.sync_cpu for debugging

assertions.name: no-scatter-evaluate
assertions.text: Does not recommend adding .evaluate() after every operation as the primary debugging approach

assertions.name: correct-evalmode-syntax
assertions.text: Uses correct context manager syntax: with ndd.EvalMode.sync_full: (not ndd.eval_mode(...) or other invented API)

assertions.name: correct-sample-inspection
assertions.text: When inspecting intermediate values, uses batch.select(i).cpu() or np.asarray(batch.select(i).cpu()) — not batch[i] or batch.as_cpu().as_array()

assertions.name: code-examples-no-pipeline-mode
assertions.text: All code examples in the guide use dynamic mode patterns (ndd.decoders.image, ndd.resize, etc.) — no fn.* or ndd.fn.* operators in any code snippet

assertions.name: code-examples-device-gpu
assertions.text: All code examples use device='gpu' for decode, NOT device='mixed'

evals.prompt: I need to train a speech classification model on WAV files using PyTorch. Write a complete Python script that uses DALI dynamic mode for the data loading and audio feature extraction (mel spectrograms). My audio clips have different durations.
evals.expected_output: Uses ndd.readers, ndd.decoders.audio(), spectral ops, handles variable-length via .torch(pad=True)

assertions.name: correct-import
assertions.text: Uses import nvidia.dali.experimental.dynamic as ndd

assertions.name: device-gpu-not-mixed
assertions.text: Uses device='gpu' for audio decode, NOT device='mixed'

assertions.name: reader-pascalcase
assertions.text: Reader class is PascalCase (e.g. ndd.readers.File)

assertions.name: reader-stateful
assertions.text: Reader created once and reused across epochs via next_epoch()

assertions.name: torch-pad-true
assertions.text: Uses .torch(pad=True) to handle variable-length spectrograms when converting to PyTorch

assertions.name: no-pipeline-mode
assertions.text: No pipeline-mode constructs (no @pipeline_def, pipe.build(), pipe.run()) and operators called directly on ndd (e.g. ndd.resize, not fn.resize or ndd.fn.resize)

evals.prompt: Write a complete Python script for an object detection training pipeline using DALI dynamic mode and PyTorch. It should read COCO-format images and annotations, apply random horizontal flip as augmentation (both images and their bounding boxes), resize, normalize, and feed to a model.
evals.expected_output: DALI reader with bbox support, coordinated augmentation via ndd.random, correct dynamic mode patterns

assertions.name: correct-import
assertions.text: Uses import nvidia.dali.experimental.dynamic as ndd

assertions.name: batch-size-to-random
assertions.text: Passes batch_size to the coin_flip/random operator

assertions.name: device-gpu-not-mixed
assertions.text: Uses device='gpu' for decode, NOT device='mixed'

assertions.name: next-epoch-iteration
assertions.text: Uses reader.next_epoch(batch_size=N) for iteration

assertions.name: torch-pad-true
assertions.text: Uses .torch(pad=True) for bounding boxes (ragged — different images have different numbers of boxes)

assertions.name: no-pipeline-mode
assertions.text: No pipeline-mode constructs (no @pipeline_def, pipe.build(), pipe.run()) and operators called directly on ndd (e.g. ndd.resize, not fn.resize or ndd.fn.resize)

assertions.name: coordinated-flip
assertions.text: The same coin_flip Batch is passed to both ndd.flip (images) and ndd.bb_flip (bounding boxes) — not two separate independent coin flips
